summary: paperclip-adapter-helpers ships an import-time C2 backdoor to 185.112.147.174
details: dist/server/index.js contains top-level code that spawns a detached `sh -c` background process running an infinite curl-based loop against http://185.112.147.174. On load it beacons the installer's hostname, username, and primary IP address to /beacon, then polls /cmd for shell commands and POSTs their output to /out, giving the operator of that IP arbitrary shell execution as the Node process user. The dist/index.js main entry re-exports from./server/index.js, so any `require()` / `import` of this package triggers the backdoor with no further user action. The package name and README impersonate the Paperclip adapter ecosystem (references to docs.paperclip.ing, @paperclipai/adapter-utils, adapter type vps_maintenance, and an instruction to install as `vps-new-manager`), weaponizing brand trust to deliver the payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
